articles.title: COMPANIES
articles.summary: Product decisions are always big bets. Do you invest in new features or quality issues? How much influence should customer requests have on your roadmap? Mistakes are costly; data is limited.

articles.title: PLG VS AI: WHAT'S NEXT FOR PRODUCT-LED GROWTH?
articles.summary: PLG isn't dying - it's evolving. AI will supercharge personalization, automate growth basics, and create agentic products that guide users, turning PLG into a co-pilot for activation, retention, and expansion.

articles.title: FORWARD DEPLOYED ENGINEERS
articles.summary: Forward Deployed Engineers embed with customers to uncover real problems and outcomes. Popularized by Palantir, this model, paired with prototyping and platform strategies, can help any team create scalable, effective products.

articles.title: SKIP-LEVEL WITHOUT A SAFETY NET
articles.summary: Skip-level readouts often overwhelm product managers, but a disciplined 3-slide structure - context, response, next steps - delivers clarity fast. This builds trust, strengthens leadership alignment, and accelerates career growth.

articles.title: I BELIEVE IN INTERIM CPO ROLES, BUT NOT IN FRACTIONAL CPO ROLES
articles.summary: Interim CPO roles can succeed, but fractional ones don't - product leadership is too unpredictable for part-time work. The most valuable task of an interim CPO is stabilizing the team and hiring their full-time successor.

articles.title: OBSERVABILITY MISSES MICRO-OUTAGES. YOUR CUSTOMERS DON'T
articles.summary: Micro outages — brief, localized issues — can silently break critical workflows without triggering alerts. The result can be broken workflows that impact revenue and customer experience, and which developers struggle to root-cause. Read the white paper [9] to learn how unitQ uses real-time customer feedback [9] to expose blind spots in your observability stack.

articles.title: TURNING INSIGHTS INTO GROWTH AND REVENUE
articles.summary: A UX research repository transforms scattered insights into a single source of truth, driving efficiency, alignment, and revenue. With AI and governance, it fuels user-centric decisions and continuous business growth.

articles.title: BRIAN ELLIOTT ON CULTURE CHAOS, RUTHLESS CLARITY, AND WHY EMPATHY FUELS VELOCITY
articles.summary: Product challenges are often cultural, not technical. Leaders should drive clarity on priorities, build dependable trust, balance performance with growth, and embed empathy to sustain velocity and innovation.

articles.title: YOUR STAKEHOLDERS AREN'T WRONG ABOUT THE PROBLEMS
articles.summary: Stakeholder intuition often signals real pain, but delivery rarely guarantees adoption. Success comes from post-launch iteration: observe users, map workflows, fix discovery, measure outcomes, and investigate time-boxes to protect investment.

articles.title: IS IT TIME TO LOOK FOR A NEW JOB? AND HOW DO I START?
articles.summary: Job doubts can create a draining limbo, but a structured search can break the cycle. With a clear plan, a strong personal pitch, and weekly time carved out, you can turn uncertainty into clarity and either re-energize in your current role or confidently step into a new one.

articles.title: AGENTS ARE BUYING, AGENTS ARE SELLING: WHAT THAT MEANS FOR GTM
articles.summary: AI agents are shifting from support roles to autonomous actors - researching, negotiating, buying, and selling.

articles.title: CURE FOR HALLUCINATIONS
articles.summary: Adding probability-based prompts helps separate imaginative output from factual certainty.
